title: escalloniaceae
intro: The family rank, Escalloniaceae, is a taxonomic grouping of flowering plants that consists of about 45 to 60 species. This family is primarily composed of shrubs and small trees, native to South America and Australia. Members of the Escalloniaceae family are known for their often fragrant flowers and distinctive leaf arrangements. They are commonly found in coastal regions and subtropical areas, where they thrive in well-drained soil and mild temperatures.
